config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Di Indonesia, saya melihat kalau “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, dan itu berisiko merugikan orang kecil. Masyarakat yang sehat seharusnya melindungi keduanya: kepentingan bersama dan hak pribadi secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should not be treated as automatically secondary to society’s interests. From a U.S. perspective, that kind of statement makes me worry about governments or majorities using “the common good” as a blanket excuse to trample free speech, due process, privacy, or property rights. That does not mean society’s interests do not matter; it means they should justify limits on individual rights only in narrow, clearly defined cases like public safety, and even then with strong safeguards. In my view, a healthy society protects the individual first precisely because that is what keeps collective power from becoming abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat dijadikan prioritas mutlak atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penggusuran, pembatasan, atau keputusan yang merugikan orang kecil tanpa perlindungan yang cukup, jadi saya justru lebih curiga pada klaim kolektif yang terlalu luas. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, terutama untuk keselamatan dan ketertiban, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar dan prosedur yang adil. Kalau tidak, “masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi alasan yang menutupi kekuasaan segelintir orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual. From a U.S. perspective, I am very wary of any broad claim that "the common good" justifies overriding speech, privacy, due process, or property rights, because history shows how easily majorities and governments can abuse that language. Society can place limits on individual rights in real emergencies or for public safety, but those limits need to be narrow, transparent, and legally checked. If we reverse the default and make the individual second, I think we invite exactly the kind of overreach that rights are supposed to prevent.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia justru “kepentingan masyarakat” sering dipakai terlalu longgar untuk menekan orang kecil. Memang ada keadaan tertentu, seperti keselamatan publik atau bencana, ketika pembatasan hak individu bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus sangat terbatas, jelas dasar hukumnya, dan ada perlindungan yang kuat. Kalau kepentingan sosial dijadikan prioritas mutlak, saya khawatir yang terjadi bukan kebaikan bersama, melainkan pembenaran bagi kekuasaan untuk mengabaikan hak warga. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga kepentingan bersama tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan hak dasar orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should not be subordinated to society as a general rule. From my U.S. perspective, I am skeptical of any broad “common good” argument because it can easily become a cover for government overreach, mob pressure, or majoritarian abuse, especially when it comes to speech, privacy, due process, and property rights. Society does have legitimate interests, but they should justify limits only in narrow situations like imminent public safety threats, with clear laws and real oversight. If we make individual rights second by default, we weaken the very protections that keep collective power from becoming oppressive.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
